milestones.vision: Deploy the KIN platform to Vercel production, verify all critical paths work end-to-end (API, web dashboard, Telegram bot), ensure the hackathon demo is polished and reliable, and harden the codebase with passing tests and type checks.
success_criteria: Web dashboard deploys to Vercel and serves at meetyourkin.com without errors
API server starts cleanly and all health endpoints respond 200
Vitest suite passes (all existing tests green)
TypeScript compiles without errors (npm run typecheck)
Telegram bot connects and responds to /start command
Free-tier chat (Groq) works end-to-end through the web UI
NFT mint flow works on Solana devnet (Stripe checkout → mint)

key_risks.risk: Vercel serverless may not support Fastify/WebSocket/SQLite patterns
key_risks.whyItMatters: The API uses Fastify with WebSocket, SQLite WAL mode, and long-lived connections — these may not work in serverless

key_risks.risk: API keys may be missing or expired for demo
key_risks.whyItMatters: 7 AI providers and multiple services (Stripe, Solana, ElevenLabs, Supermemory) all need valid keys for the demo

key_risks.risk: Next.js build may fail due to SSR/client boundary issues
key_risks.whyItMatters: The web app uses Three.js, Framer Motion, and browser-only APIs that can break SSR

key_risks.risk: Existing tests may not pass on current codebase
key_risks.whyItMatters: Tests were written during development and may have drifted from the implementation

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Next.js web app builds and deploys to Vercel; API deployment strategy validated
All Vitest tests pass, TypeScript compiles cleanly
User can chat with a companion through the web UI using Groq free tier

milestones.verification_contract: All code changes verified by: npm test (Vitest), npm run typecheck (TSC), and manual smoke test via scripts/smoke.ts
milestones.verification_integration: Cross-slice integration verified by end-to-end flow: web UI → API → inference → response displayed
milestones.verification_operational: Production deployment verified by Vercel build logs + live URL health check
milestones.verification_uat: Demo walkthrough: landing page → onboarding → chat with companion → verify response quality
definition_of_done: npm run typecheck passes with 0 errors
cd web && npm run build succeeds
Vercel deployment URL loads without errors
Smoke test (npx tsx scripts/smoke.ts) passes all checks
milestones.requirement_coverage: R001 (Vercel deploy) → S01; R002 (test health) → S02; R003 (e2e chat) → S03
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
```
S01 (Web Build & Deploy) → provides: deployed URL, build config
S02 (Test & Type Health)  → provides: green test suite, clean types
S03 (E2E Chat Validation) → requires: S01 (deployed URL), S02 (stable codebase)
```

slices.title: Web App Build Fix & Vercel Deployment
slices.risk: high — SSR/client boundary issues, Vercel config unknowns
slices.demo: After this, the Next.js web app builds cleanly and deploys to Vercel
slices.goal: Fix any Next.js build errors and deploy the web dashboard to Vercel production
slices.success_criteria: cd web && npm run build succeeds; Vercel deploy completes; site loads at production URL
slices.integration_closure: Web app serves as the frontend for the Fastify API
slices.observability_impact: Vercel build logs + deployment URL accessible

slices.title: Test Suite & TypeScript Health
slices.risk: medium — tests may have drifted from implementation
slices.demo: After this, npm test and npm run typecheck both pass cleanly
slices.goal: Fix all failing tests and TypeScript errors so the codebase is provably healthy
slices.success_criteria: npm test exits 0; npm run typecheck exits 0; no skipped tests without justification
slices.integration_closure: Healthy test suite validates API, bot, inference, and voice modules
slices.observability_impact: CI-ready test suite provides regression safety net

slices.title: End-to-End Chat Validation
slices.risk: medium — requires API keys and running services
slices.demo: After this, a user can open the web app and have a real conversation with a KIN companion
slices.goal: Validate the full chat flow from web UI through API to inference and back
slices.success_criteria: Free-tier user can send a message and receive a streaming response; conversation persists in SQLite
slices.integration_closure: Proves the full stack works: Next.js → Fastify → Groq → SSE → UI
slices.observability_impact: Chat latency and provider routing logged via trajectory system

tasks.title: Audit & fix Next.js build
tasks.description: Run `cd web && npm run build` and fix all errors — SSR/client boundary mismatches, missing imports, Three.js dynamic imports, type errors. Goal: clean build with 0 errors.
expected_output: Clean Next.js build output in web/.next/

tasks.title: Verify Vercel configuration
tasks.description: Check web/vercel.json, ensure build settings are correct, validate environment variables are listed, confirm the project can be deployed from the web/ directory.
expected_output: Validated vercel.json with correct build config

tasks.title: Run smoke test against local API
tasks.description: Start the API server locally and run the smoke test script to verify all health and critical endpoints respond correctly.
expected_output: Smoke test output showing all checks passing

tasks.title: Fix TypeScript compilation errors
tasks.description: Run `npm run typecheck` and fix all TypeScript errors across the entire backend codebase (api/, bot/, inference/, runtime/, voice/, scripts/).

tasks.title: Fix failing Vitest tests
tasks.description: Run `npm test` and fix all failing Vitest tests. Update test expectations where the implementation has intentionally changed. Keep test coverage at current level or better.

tasks.title: E2E chat flow validation
tasks.description: Start the API server and web dev server. Open the web app in browser. Navigate to chat, send a test message, verify the companion responds with streaming tokens. Confirm the conversation is saved to SQLite.
tasks.verify: Manual: open http://localhost:3001, navigate to chat, send message, verify streaming response

decisions.choice: Initialize with M001 focused on production deployment readiness (Vercel), as the core platform is hackathon-complete and the README roadmap lists Vercel deployment as the immediate next step
decisions.rationale: The codebase is mature — 50+ API endpoints, 25+ pages, 7 AI providers, multi-platform bots, NFT minting, skills marketplace, and 17+ tests are all implemented. The project is for the Bags Hackathon Q1 2026 and needs production deployment to demonstrate the live product. GSD tracking will organize the remaining deployment, IPFS, and on-chain anchoring work.
